title: Saccolabium secundum
intro: Saccolabium secundum, also known as the "tongue orchid," is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Saccolabium secundum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections and other conditions.
